Went on a Tuesday for my gf’s bday and it was overall fun! Since we went on a weekday it wasn’t too busy and it was easy to walk through the zoo with the handy SF Zoo app. All the animals were all fun to observe and to just see how they go on with their day! One exhibit would be lively while the other would be more calm and relaxing. For example the gorillas were the most active out of the bunch by running around and interacting with each other while the lions were primarily resting! There were some animals that would sleep hidden from our views but we didn’t mind at all! Overall we would go again! They are also making a dinosaur exhibit so that gives us another reason to visit! :)

Many unusual animals, some critically endangered. We were lucky to watch the Red Panda running around her fantastic treehouse, very active and adorable. Learned amazing things from Docents in various locations. Best: the Giant Anteater! A fun place for kids, but even better for adults wanting to learn more about the animals.

I love a good zoo and try to visit where ever i am on holiday but i will say this one disappointed me, glad i didn’t pay outright and it was part of my CityPass ticket. 
Its a large zoo but it became a running joke about how many enclosures were empty…not like for maintenance or coming soon, i’m talking unkept and empty for years type empty. 
You can tell covid hit the zoo hard and is struggling financially, its a shame cos i bet years ago it was amazing. 
Don’t get me wrong, i saw some rarer animal species i’d never seen before which was amazing so its not all bad. 
The food at the main cafe was also really good, i was surprised. 
Toilets were gross though. 
I think they charged something crazy like $9 to ride the mini train that went in a small loop. 
I’m still glad i went but i wouldn’t go again or recommend it to anyone.
